name: Countybank
description_short: Regional community bank serving Upstate South Carolina with comprehensive personal and business banking, lending, mortgages, insurance, and wealth management services.
description_long:
Countybank is a regional community bank based in Greenville, South Carolina, with branches throughout the Upstate region including Anderson and surrounding areas. As an FDIC member institution, deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing standard federal deposit insurance protection.

The bank offers a full suite of consumer and business financial services. Personal products include multiple checking account tiers (Classic, Cash Back, Signature, Premier), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, HSAs, auto loans, personal loans, HELOCs, and personal credit cards. Business customers can access free and interest-bearing checking, business savings, business money market accounts, business CDs, custom business loans, business lines of credit, SBA loans, business credit cards, cash management services, remote deposit capture, and merchant services. Countybank also operates a mortgage division handling home purchases, refinancing, and construction financing, along with Countybanc Insurance (auto, home, life, commercial) and a dedicated wealth management division including trust services, estate planning, investment management, financial planning, and retirement planning through its Greenwood Capital subsidiary.

Countybank differentiates itself through local presence and community involvement, as evidenced by the Countybank Foundation's charitable donations and community partnerships. The bank emphasizes personalized service with direct phone support (800-726-8689) and maintains dedicated teams for mortgages, insurance, trust services, and investments. Their digital banking platform and mobile wallet offerings provide modern convenience alongside traditional relationship banking.

As a regional community bank, Countybank's primary advantage lies in local decision-making and personalized service rather than the technology infrastructure or rates of larger national banks. The broad range of services under one institution provides convenience for customers seeking multiple financial products, though rates and fees should be compared with national competitors. No specific account minimums, fees, or APR information is publicly detailed on the homepage.
services: Personal checking accounts (Classic, Cash Back, Signature, Premier, Student)
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for personal and business customers
Personal loans and Instant Cash Line of Credit
Business loans and custom business lines of credit
Wealth management and trust services including living trusts, charitable trusts, and special needs trusts
Insurance products (auto, home, life, commercial) through Countybanc Insurance
Cash management and remote deposit capture for businesses
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full federal backing
Multiple checking account options (6+ tiers) serving different customer needs from students to premier customers
Comprehensive mortgage division with dedicated mortgage team and pre-approval support
Full-service wealth management including trust services, estate planning, and investment management
Integrated insurance services through Countybanc Insurance subsidiary
Business-focused services including SBA loan programs and cash management tools
Active community involvement through Countybank Foundation and documented charitable giving
Multiple digital banking options including mobile wallet and remote deposit capture
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Upstate South Carolina region only
No published interest rates, APRs, or account fees on website—requires direct inquiry
No online account opening or application process visible; appears to require branch visit
Small regional institution may lack the 24/7 live chat or AI support of larger banks
Limited transparency on loan approval criteria and qualification requirements
best_for: Small business owners in Upstate South Carolina seeking relationship banking and SBA financing
Individuals in the Greenville/Anderson area wanting comprehensive banking, mortgage, and wealth management under one institution
High-net-worth individuals and families seeking trust services, estate planning, and investment management
Customers prioritizing local decision-making and personalized service over online-only banking convenience
diagnosis: Countybank is best suited for individuals and small business owners in the Upstate South Carolina region who value local relationship banking and need multiple financial services under one institution. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this is a regional bank with no out-of-state presence, and online rate/fee transparency is minimal, requiring direct contact with local branches for specific product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
